title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Origins of the Public Museum
content.text: The modern concept of the public museum (0) emerged during the Enlightenment, a period that valued the collection and categorization of knowledge. Before this, most rare objects were (1) in private 'cabinets of curiosities' owned by wealthy aristocrats. These collections were often (2) for a small, elite audience and served as a status symbol. However, the opening of the British Museum in 1759 (3) a significant shift toward the democratization of culture. For the first time, people from different social backgrounds were (4) to view artifacts from across the globe. The goal of these early institutions was not just to display treasures, but to (5) the public through the study of history and science. Over the years, the role of the museum has continued to (6) , with a greater focus on interactive and digital experiences. Today, museums are seen as (7) spaces for community engagement and critical thinking. They provide a unique opportunity for us to (8) with our shared past and imagine a common future. Despite the rise of online archives, the physical presence of a historical object remains a powerful tool for education, proving that the tangible connection to our heritage is essential for understanding the complexities of human civilization and the diverse cultures that shape our world.

example.explanation: “Emerged” is used to describe the appearance or development of a new idea or concept.

questions.explanation: “Housed” is a formal verb for providing a space for a collection or group of objects.
“Intended for” is used to describe the target audience or purpose of something.
“Marked a shift” is a common collocation for indicating a turning point or change.
“Permitted” is a formal verb for being allowed to do something.
“Enlighten” is a formal verb meaning to provide someone with greater knowledge and understanding.
“Evolve” describes the gradual and continuous development of an institution or system.
“Vital spaces” describes places that are essential for the health or success of a community.
“Connect with” is the standard way to describe forming a mental or emotional link.
